extracted_json.title: Step 5: Relationships Between Players in the I/M Landscape (Intel Case Study)

blocks.text: Comment: Competitive strategy is dynamic (evolutionary) ⟹ companies change their competitive strategy with time ⟹ the creation of functional maps guides the evolution of strategy
Step 5: Determine the relationships between the different sets of players in the I/M landscape
Example: The Intel Case Study (HW#2, Prob#2)

items.text: What relationships did Intel (the competitor in the microprocessor industry) have with its suppliers?
What relationships have with its buyers (i.e., computer manufacturers)

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Step 5 of strategy analysis: determining relationships between players in the I/M landscape, illustrated by the Intel case study.
extracted_json.summary_gist: The page notes that competitive strategy is dynamic and evolutionary, with functional maps guiding its evolution. Step 5 is to determine the relationships between different sets of players in the I/M landscape. The Intel Case Study (HW#2, Prob#2) is used as an example, examining Intel's relationships with its suppliers (e.g., single sourcing) and with its buyers/computer manufacturers (e.g., the "Intel Inside" campaign and WINTEL).
